header_oversize.description: Cache DoS via HTTP Header Oversize (HHO) -- send oversized headers that trigger error responses cached by intermediary

probes.description: Oversized X-Forwarded-For header -- send extremely long XFF header to trigger 400/431 error that gets cached, denying service to all users requesting same URL
Oversized Cookie header for cache DoS -- large Cookie header triggers 400 error cached for all users

cache_key_normalization.description: Cache key normalization DoS -- exploit differences in URL normalization between cache and origin to poison cache with error responses

probes.description: Null byte in URL for cache DoS -- cache normalizes away null byte (same key as clean URL), origin returns 400, cached error serves to all users
CRLF in URL path for cache DoS -- cache strips CRLF when computing key, origin rejects with error, error is cached for clean URL

vary_header_abuse.description: Vary header abuse for cache DoS -- manipulate Vary header to fragment cache or cause cache misses

probes.description: Inject X-Forwarded-Host to influence Vary -- if server adds injected header to Vary response, cache fragments per unique value, reducing cache hit rate (DoS via cache fragmentation)
Vary: * detection -- server returning Vary: * makes response uncacheable, potential amplification if attacker can trigger this

large_response_caching.description: Cache DoS via large response -- trigger large error responses that consume cache storage or bandwidth

probes.description: Large response via inflated limit parameter -- trigger oversized response that consumes cache storage when cached
